title: The Art of War
author: Sun Tzu
content: “In the midst of chaos, there is also opportunity.”
action: Context: The strategic shift required when the environment becomes non-deterministic.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
content: “Actual happiness always looks pretty squalid in comparison with the overcompensations for misery.”
action: Context: The danger of replacing authentic, challenging reality with optimized, artificial comfort.

title: Walden Two
author: B.F. Skinner
content: “The real problem is not whether machines think but whether men do.”
action: Context: The diagnostic check on whether individual agency is being utilized or outsourced.

title: The Myth of Sisyphus
author: Albert Camus
content: “The struggle itself toward the heights is enough to fill a man's heart. One must imagine Sisyphus happy.”
action: Context: The adoption of the task itself—regardless of result—as the primary source of meaning.

title: Meditations
author: Marcus Aurelius
content: “Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.”
action: Context: The immediate move from conceptual deliberation to operational output.
